title: alectra rigida subsp. paludosa
intro: Alectra rigida subsp. paludosa, also known as swamp alectra, is a plant species in the family Orobanchaceae, and is a type of hemiparasitic herb that obtains some of its nutrients by parasitizing the roots of other plants. Traditionally, the plant has been used to treat various health issues, including fever, rheumatism, and skin conditions, due to its medicinal properties. The health benefits of swamp alectra include its anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties, which can help to reduce swelling and fight off infections. Swamp alectra can be prepared as a decoction or infusion, and is often used in traditional medicine in various parts of the world, although its use should be under the guidance of a medical professional.
